title: An Optimal Algorithm for Stopping on the Element Closest to the Center of an Interval
author_string: Ewa M. Kubicka, Grzegorz Kubicki, Małgorzata Kuchta, Małgorzata Sulkowska
abstract: Real numbers from the interval [0, 1] are randomly selected with uniform distribution. There are $n$ of them and they are revealed one by one. However, we do not know their values but only their relative ranks. We want to stop on recently revealed number maximizing the probability that that number is closest to $\frac{1}{2}$. We design an optimal stopping algorithm achieving our goal and prove that its probability of success is asymptotically equivalent to $\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\sqrt{\frac{2}π}$.
